purpose: Cross-family (different model FAMILY) independence check on the Router-V2 blind labeling, hardening the same-family shared-prior residual (HON-HIGH-2 / HON-PR2). NOT folded into the N=3 Claude ensemble; a separate disclosed metric.
finding: route labels are 89% family-independent (a different model family confirms them); root/borderline are the contested zone where GPT escalates MORE than Claude (root->borderline). Both families are route-heavy => the under-scoring signal is robust across families, not a same-family artifact. The corpus-bias (substrate own board spawns) remains; cross-family agreement narrows the shared-prior residual, it does not close the corpus-bias.
caveats: Vendor-bound text was redacted by the committed scrub.js (the absolute /Users/<name>/ home path -> ~, dropping the OS username; temp roots -> <tmp>). VERIFIABLE: the same scrub.js scrubs the committed route-eval-set.jsonl, which carries zero home paths (grep -c /Users/ -> 0); no secret-shaped tokens were present in the corpus, only the username-in-path PII.
GPT and the Claude ensemble share neither weights nor vendor, but both are LLM labelers reading the same truncated prefixes (the truncation circle is NOT broken by cross-family).
contested rows excluded from the agreement (no Claude majority).
